iterations.theme: Uloga Mosta u hrvatskoj politici i izazovi izbornog sustava

sections.subtitle: Uvod i predstavljanje Ivana Orlovića
sections.screenshot_description: Matija predstavlja Ivana Orlovića, organizacijskog tajnika Mosta.
sections.content: U uvodnom dijelu podcasta, voditelj Matija srdačno je pozdravio svog gosta, **Ivana Orlovića**, predstavljajući ga kao organizacijskog tajnika Mosta i osobnog prijatelja. Matija je izrazio zadovoljstvo što je Ivan izdvojio vrijeme za razgovor, najavljujući teme koje će obuhvatiti njegov osobni i politički put, kao i ulogu Mosta u hrvatskoj politici.
sections.subtitle_en: Introduction and presentation of Ivan Orlović
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matija presents Ivan Orlović, the organizational secretary of Most.
sections.content_en: In the introductory part of the podcast, host Matija cordially greeted his guest, **Ivan Orlović**, introducing him as the organizational secretary of Most and a personal friend. Matija expressed satisfaction that Ivan took the time for a conversation, announcing topics that will cover his personal and political journey, as well as the role of Most in Croatian politics.

sections.subtitle: Ivan Orlović: Osobni i politički put
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović govori o svom osobnom i političkom putu, naglašavajući svoju ulogu u Mostu.
sections.content: Ivan Orlović predstavio se kao 34-godišnji suprug i otac troje djece, po struci inženjer računalnih mreža. U struci je radio do prije nekoliko mjeseci, a politički je angažiran od kraja 2018. godine kroz stranku Most. Početkom tekuće godine ukazana mu je čast da postane organizacijski tajnik Mosta, što je prihvatio s ciljem da stranku organizacijski unaprijedi. Iako je naslijedio solidnu situaciju, smatra da uvijek ima prostora za poboljšanje u svakom poslu, pa tako i u organizacijskom smislu unutar stranke.
sections.subtitle_en: Ivan Orlović: Personal and political path
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović speaks about his personal and political path, emphasizing his role in Most.
sections.content_en: Ivan Orlović introduced himself as a 34-year-old husband and father of three children, by profession a computer network engineer. He worked in his profession until a few months ago, and has been politically active since the end of 2018 through the Most party. At the beginning of the current year, he was honored to become the organizational secretary of Most, which he accepted with the aim of organizationally improving the party. Although he inherited a solid situation, he believes that there is always room for improvement in every job, including in an organizational sense within the party.

sections.subtitle: Uloga organizacijskog tajnika Mosta
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović detaljno objašnjava širok spektar odgovornosti organizacijskog tajnika Mosta.
sections.content: Uloga organizacijskog tajnika Mosta, kako objašnjava Ivan Orlović, iznimno je bitna funkcija prema statutu stranke. To je pozicija koja nosi i politički faktor, s obzirom na to da je organizacijski tajnik član predsjedništva i sudjeluje u kreiranju stranačkih politika. Što se tiče organizacijskog dijela, spektar odgovornosti je vrlo širok. Orlović rukovodi kompletnim ljudstvom i zaposlenicima, koordinira rad svih ureda, uključujući Središnji ured, te je odgovoran za financije, sve pravne akte, reviziju i trošenje javnog novca, sve to u suradnji s predsjednikom stranke.
sections.subtitle_en: Role of the organizational secretary of Mosta
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović explains in detail the wide spectrum of responsibilities of the organizational secretary of Most.
sections.content_en: The role of the organizational secretary of Most, as Ivan Orlović explains, is an extremely important function according to the party's statute. It is a position that also carries a political factor, given that the organizational secretary is a member of the presidency and participates in the creation of party policies. Regarding the organizational part, the spectrum of responsibilities is very wide. Orlović manages the entire staff and employees, coordinates the work of all offices, including the Central Office, and is responsible for finances, all legal acts, auditing, and the spending of public money, all in cooperation with the party president.

sections.subtitle: Mostova organizacijska struktura i uredi
sections.screenshot_description: Prikaz Mostovih ureda diljem Hrvatske i planova za širenje organizacijske mreže.
sections.content: Most trenutno ima razvijenu organizacijsku strukturu s nekoliko ureda diljem Hrvatske. Središnji ured nalazi se u Zagrebu, a ostali uredi su u Splitu, Osijeku, Zaprešiću, Velikoj Gorici, Koprivnici i Metkoviću. Ivan Orlović ističe da je u tijeku proces osnivanja organizacija u svim županijama, s ciljem da do kraja godine Most ima urede u svim županijama, odnosno u najvećim gradovima tih županija. Sljedeći ured koji će biti otvoren je u Rijeci. Uz Ivana Orlovića, u stranci je zaposleno još sedam ljudi, uključujući dvije osobe u PR i digitalnom timu, dva regionalna koordinatora (za zagrebačku regiju te Rijeku, Kvarner, Istru i Gorski Kotar), zaposlenicu u splitskom uredu te regionalnog koordinatora za Dubrovačko-neretvansku županiju sa sjedištem u Metkoviću.
sections.subtitle_en: Most's organizational structure and offices
sections.screenshot_description_en: Overview of Most's offices across Croatia and plans for the expansion of the organizational network.
sections.content_en: Most currently has a developed organizational structure with several offices throughout Croatia. The central office is located in Zagreb, and other offices are in Split, Osijek, Zaprešić, Velika Gorica, Koprivnica, and Metković. Ivan Orlović points out that the process of establishing organizations in all counties is underway, with the aim that by the end of the year Most will have offices in all counties, i.e., in the largest cities of those counties. The next office to be opened is in Rijeka. In addition to Ivan Orlović, seven more people are employed in the party, including two people in the PR and digital team, two regional coordinators (for the Zagreb region and Rijeka, Kvarner, Istria, and Gorski Kotar), an employee in the Split office, and a regional coordinator for the Dubrovnik-Neretva County with headquarters in Metković.

sections.subtitle: Financiranje političkih stranaka
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović objašnjava sustav financiranja političkih stranaka iz državnog proračuna i lokalne samouprave.
sections.content: Matija je postavio pitanje o sustavnom financiranju političkih stranaka u Hrvatskoj. Ivan Orlović pojašnjava da se financiranje odvija prema Zakonu o financiranju političkih stranaka. Dio sredstava dolazi iz državnog proračuna, ovisno o broju saborskih zastupnika, dok se drugi dio financira iz lokalne samouprave, proporcionalno broju lokalnih vijećnika, županijskih zastupnika i gradskih vijećnika. Orlović navodi da je prosječna godišnja cifra oko **70.000 eura po saborskom zastupniku**, što su javno dostupni podaci. Zanimljivo je da postoji i dodatna subvencija od 10% za angažman žena u politici. Most trenutno ima sedam zastupnika, a u njihovom klubu je i nezavisni zastupnik Josip Jurčević, što čini ukupno osam. Orlović pojašnjava da profesor Jurčević, iako je dio kluba, nije bio na Mostovoj listi 2020. godine, već je ušao preko liste Domovinskog pokreta, te se kasnije priključio Mostovom klubu nakon što Domovinski pokret nije podržao HDZ. Stoga, administrativno, njegov prihod ne ide izravno Mostu.
sections.subtitle_en: Financing of political parties
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović explains the system of financing political parties from the state budget and local self-government.
sections.content_en: Matija asked a question about the systematic financing of political parties in Croatia. Ivan Orlović explains that financing takes place according to the Law on the Financing of Political Parties. Part of the funds comes from the state budget, depending on the number of parliamentary representatives, while the other part is financed by local self-government, proportional to the number of local councilors, county representatives, and city councilors. Orlović states that the average annual figure is around **70,000 euros per parliamentary representative**, which is publicly available data. It is interesting that there is also an additional subsidy of 10% for the engagement of women in politics. Most currently has seven representatives, and independent representative Josip Jurčević is also in their club, making a total of eight. Orlović explains that Professor Jurčević, although part of the club, was not on Most's list in 2020, but entered through the list of Domovinski pokret, and later joined Most's club after Domovinski pokret did not support HDZ. Therefore, administratively, his income does not go directly to Most.
entities_en: Law on the Financing of Political Parties

sections.subtitle: Procedura za izlazak na parlamentarne izbore
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović objašnjava jednostavnost procedure za formiranje izborne liste za parlamentarne izbore.
sections.content: Matija je izrazio interes za proceduru izlaska na parlamentarne izbore za nezavisne liste. Ivan Orlović je pojasnio da za parlamentarne izbore **ne postoje zahtjevi za prikupljanjem potpisa**. Dovoljno je da netko osnuje stranku i izađe u tri izborne jedinice. Ključno je imati 14 ljudi po izbornoj jedinici koji nisu kažnjavani i koji su spremni stati na listu za Sabor. Jedini uvjet je potvrda o nekažnjavanju. To znači da praktički bilo kojih četrnaest ljudi u Hrvatskoj može formirati svoju izbornu listu za jednu izbornu jedinicu, bez obzira na to gdje su prijavljeni.
sections.subtitle_en: Procedure for going to parliamentary elections
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović explains the simplicity of the procedure for forming an electoral list for parliamentary elections.
sections.content_en: Matija expressed interest in the procedure for participating in parliamentary elections for independent lists. Ivan Orlović clarified that for parliamentary elections **there are no requirements for collecting signatures**. It is enough for someone to found a party and run in three electoral units. It is crucial to have 14 people per electoral unit who have not been convicted and who are ready to stand on the list for the Parliament. The only condition is a certificate of no criminal record. This means that practically any fourteen people in Croatia can form their electoral list for one electoral unit, regardless of where they are registered.

sections.subtitle: Kandidati izvan matičnih izbornih jedinica
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović objašnjava zašto kandidati mogu biti učinkoviti i izvan svoje matične izborne jedinice, koristeći primjer Zvonimira Troskota.
sections.content: Matija je postavio pitanje o kandidatima koji se kandidiraju u izbornim jedinicama u kojima nemaju prebivalište. Ivan Orlović smatra da to nije nužno loše. Kao primjer navodi **Zvonimira Troskota** iz Mosta, koji je Zagrepčanin, ali godinama terenski djeluje u sedmoj izbornoj jedinici. Troskot se bavio temama poput Kosinjskog polja, gdje su se ljudi protivili iseljavanju zbog akumulacijskog jezera, te je otkrio problem otrovnog otpada u Lici. Orlović ističe da je Troskotov dugogodišnji angažman u toj jedinici prirodan razlog za njegovu kandidaturu tamo, bez obzira na mjesto prebivališta. Zaključuje da, ako se netko posvetio ljudima iz određene izborne jedinice, nije loše da bude njihov kandidat, čak i ako nije iz tog kraja.
sections.subtitle_en: Candidates outside native electoral units
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović explains why candidates can be effective even outside their home electoral unit, using the example of Zvonimir Troskot.
sections.content_en: Matija asked a question about candidates who run in electoral units where they do not have residence. Ivan Orlović believes that this is not necessarily bad. As an example, he cites **Zvonimir Troskot** from Most, who is a Zagrepčanin, but has been working in the field for years in the seventh electoral unit. Troskot dealt with topics such as Kosinjsko polje, where people opposed displacement due to the accumulation lake, and he discovered the problem of toxic waste in Lika. Orlović points out that Troskot's long-standing engagement in that unit is a natural reason for his candidacy there, regardless of the place of residence. He concludes that, if someone has dedicated themselves to the people from a certain electoral unit, it is not bad for them to be their candidate, even if they are not from that area.

sections.subtitle: Prijedlozi za unaprjeđenje izbornog sustava
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović iznosi svoje prijedloge za reformu izbornog sustava, uključujući jednu izbornu jedinicu i niži prag.
sections.content: Ivan Orlović iznio je svoje prijedloge za unaprjeđenje izbornog sustava u Hrvatskoj. Smatra da je trenutni sustav s prevelikim brojem neuravnoteženih izbornih jedinica loš. Njegov osobni stav je da bi bilo najbolje da je **cijela država jedna izborna jedinica**, čime bi se uklonile geografske granice. Također, predlaže da se omogući biračima da daju barem dva, ako ne i tri, preferencijalna glasa te da se izborni prag spusti s 5% na 3%. Orlović vjeruje da bi ove promjene značajno unaprijedile parlamentarnu demokraciju. Iako priznaje važnost premijera kao čelnika izvršne vlasti, ističe da je potreba za većinom od 76 ruku u Saboru ipak demokratičnija od sustava gdje jedna osoba ima preveliku moć, kao što je slučaj u predsjedničkim sustavima poput Francuske ili SAD-a.
sections.subtitle_en: Proposals for the improvement of the electoral system
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović presents his proposals for the reform of the electoral system, including one electoral unit and a lower threshold.
sections.content_en: Ivan Orlović presented his proposals for improving the electoral system in Croatia. He believes that the current system with too many unbalanced electoral units is bad. His personal view is that it would be best if the **entire country were one electoral unit**, which would remove geographical borders. Also, he proposes that voters be allowed to cast at least two, if not three, preferential votes and that the electoral threshold be lowered from 5% to 3%. Orlović believes that these changes would significantly improve parliamentary democracy. Although he acknowledges the importance of the prime minister as the head of the executive power, he points out that the need for a majority of 76 hands in the Sabor is still more democratic than a system where one person has too much power, as is the case in presidential systems like France or the USA.

sections.subtitle: Prednosti i nedostaci postojećeg izbornog sustava
sections.screenshot_description: Matija i Ivan Orlović raspravljaju o ograničenjima postojećeg izbornog sustava i uspoređuju ga s izborima za Europski parlament.
sections.content: Matija se složio s idejom jedne izborne jedinice za cijelu državu, ističući apsurd da ne može glasati za političara iz druge izborne jedinice, bez obzira na to koliko ga cijeni. Ivan Orlović potvrdio je da je to najveći nedostatak trenutnog sustava, jer ograničava birače na mali geografski prostor i 14 kandidata. Smatra da bi jedna izborna jedinica omogućila biračima da glasaju za bilo kojeg kandidata iz bilo kojeg dijela Hrvatske, primjerice iz Vukovara, Dubrovnika ili Splita. Orlović je spomenuo da je za izbore za Europski parlament Hrvatska jedna izborna jedinica, ali da tu postoji drugi problem – **vrlo niska izlaznost** (manje od 30%). Zbog toga, unatoč prednosti jedne izborne jedinice, rezultati nisu mjerodavni. Naglašava da su europski izbori podcijenjeni, iako Europska komisija donosi zakone koji značajno utječu na život građana, a javnost ih često percipira samo kao priliku za visoke plaće zastupnika.
sections.subtitle_en: Advantages and disadvantages of the existing electoral system
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matija and Ivan Orlović discuss the limitations of the existing electoral system and compare it with the elections for the European Parliament.
sections.content_en: Matija agreed with the idea of a single electoral unit for the entire country, highlighting the absurdity that he cannot vote for a politician from another electoral unit, regardless of how much he values them. Ivan Orlović confirmed that this is the biggest drawback of the current system, as it limits voters to a small geographical area and 14 candidates. He believes that a single electoral unit would allow voters to vote for any candidate from any part of Croatia, for example from Vukovar, Dubrovnik, or Split. Orlović mentioned that for the European Parliament elections, Croatia is a single electoral unit, but that there is another problem – **very low turnout** (less than 30%). Because of this, despite the advantage of a single electoral unit, the results are not authoritative. He emphasizes that European elections are underestimated, even though the European Commission enacts laws that significantly affect the lives of citizens, and the public often perceives them only as an opportunity for high salaries for representatives.

sections.subtitle: Razočaranje politikom i važnost dosljednosti
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović naglašava potrebu za dosljednim i poštenim političarima kako bi se vratilo povjerenje građana u politiku.
sections.content: Ivan Orlović ističe da su ljudi u Hrvatskoj, nažalost i s razlogom, razočarani politikom. Mnogi političari su ih razočarali i kompromitirali, što je dovelo do gubitka vjere u ljude i u politiku općenito. Orlović smatra da se ta situacija može promijeniti samo s političarima koji su **dosljedni, pošteni i vjerni onome što govore**. Takvi političari ne smiju biti potkupljivi niti na prodaju. Vjeruje da je to ključno za vraćanje povjerenja građana i poboljšanje političke scene u zemlji.
sections.subtitle_en: Disappointment with politics and the importance of consistency
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović emphasizes the need for consistent and honest politicians to restore citizens' trust in politics.
sections.content_en: Ivan Orlović points out that people in Croatia are, unfortunately and with reason, disappointed with politics. Many politicians have disappointed and compromised them, which has led to a loss of faith in people and in politics generally. Orlović believes that this situation can only change with politicians who are **consistent, honest, and faithful to what they say**. Such politicians must not be corruptible nor for sale. He believes that this is crucial for restoring citizens' trust and improving the political scene in the country.

sections.subtitle: Matematički nedostaci izbornog sustava
sections.screenshot_description: Matija i Ivan Orlović analiziraju matematičke anomalije izbornog sustava, posebno D'Hondtovu metodu i izborni prag.
sections.content: Matija je ilustrirao matematičke nedostatke izbornog sustava hipotetskim primjerom: u izbornoj jedinici s 20 stranaka, 19 ih osvoji 999 glasova, a 20. stranka 1000 glasova. Samo ta 20. stranka, s nešto više od 5% glasova, prolazi izborni prag i, prema D'Hondtovoj metodi, osvaja svih 14 mandata. Ivan Orlović se složio da je ta **neproporcionalnost glasa**, gdje svaki glas ne vrijedi jednako, najveći krimen ovakvog sustava. Smatra da bi se takve situacije izbjegle kada bi cijela Hrvatska bila jedna izborna jedinica, a prag smanjen na 3%. Podsjetio je da je Most 2020. godine u petoj izbornoj jedinici, unatoč tome što je prešao prag s 5.5% glasova, ostao bez mandata zbog D'Hondtove metode. Zaključuje da je kombinacija više izbornih jedinica i visokog praga problematična.
sections.subtitle_en: Mathematical shortcomings of the electoral system
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matija and Ivan Orlović analyze mathematical anomalies of the electoral system, especially the D'Hondt method and the electoral threshold.
sections.content_en: Matija illustrated the mathematical shortcomings of the electoral system with a hypothetical example: in an electoral unit with 20 parties, 19 of them win 999 votes, and the 20th party 1000 votes. Only that 20th party, with slightly more than 5% of the votes, passes the electoral threshold and, according to the D'Hondt method, wins all 14 mandates. Ivan Orlović agreed that this **disproportionality of the vote**, where every vote is not worth equally, is the biggest crime of such a system. He believes that such situations would be avoided if the whole of Hrvatska were one electoral unit, and the threshold reduced to 3%. He reminded that Most in 2020 in the fifth electoral unit, despite having crossed the threshold with 5.5% of the votes, remained without mandates due to the D'Hondt method. He concludes that the combination of multiple electoral units and a high threshold is problematic.

sections.subtitle: Zašto se izborni sustav ne mijenja
sections.screenshot_description: Matija postavlja pitanje o otporu promjenama izbornog sustava, unatoč dostupnosti modernih tehnologija za transparentnost.
sections.content: Matija je postavio ključno pitanje zašto se izborni sustav u Hrvatskoj ne mijenja, unatoč dostupnosti modernih tehnologija poput blockchaina i naprednih osobnih iskaznica koje bi mogle osigurati transparentnost i minimalizirati malverzacije. Ističe da je Hrvatska osobna iskaznica jedan od najboljih tehnoloških identifikacijskih dokumenata u svijetu, s visokom razinom kriptografije. Međutim, primjećuje da svaka vlast, jednom kada dođe na poziciju, rijetko inicira promjene izbornog sustava. Matija zaključuje da je to vjerojatno zato što im trenutni sustav odgovara, omogućujući im da ostanu na vlasti. Postavlja pitanje zašto je to tako i kakva je percepcija tog otpora promjenama.
sections.subtitle_en: Why isn't the electoral system changing?
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matija asks a question about resistance to changes of the electoral system, despite the availability of modern technologies for transparency.
sections.content_en: Matija asked a key question why the electoral system in Croatia is not changing, despite the availability of modern technologies such as blockchain and advanced personal identification cards that could ensure transparency and minimize malversations. He points out that the Croatian personal identification card is one of the best technological identification documents in the world, with a high level of cryptography. However, he notes that every government, once it comes to power, rarely initiates changes to the electoral system. Matija concludes that this is probably because the current system suits them, allowing them to remain in power. He asks why this is so and what is the perception of that resistance to change.

sections.subtitle: Otpor vladajućih promjenama izbornog zakona
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović objašnjava zašto HDZ i SDP ne žele mijenjati izborni zakon i brani dosljednost Mosta u političkom djelovanju.
sections.content: Ivan Orlović objašnjava da velikim strankama, poput **HDZ-a i SDP-a**, trenutni izborni sustav odgovara jer je krojen njima u korist još od devedesetih godina. Smatra da bi bilo 'budalasto' mijenjati nešto što im donosi dobre rezultate. Orlović navodi dvije propuštene prilike za promjenu izbornog zakona: prva je bila inicijativa udruge (u kojoj je sudjelovao i Zvonimir Troskot prije političke karijere) za referendum o promjeni izbornog zakona (dva-tri preferencijalna glasa, prag od 3%), koja je propala zbog odluke Ustavnog suda o 'nečitkim potpisima', što naziva 'krađom referenduma'. Druga prilika bila je 2020. godine kada je Most pozvao Domovinski pokret da ne koalira s HDZ-om, već da se formira saborska većina za promjenu izbornog zakona i prijevremene izbore, što Domovinski pokret nije prihvatio. Orlović kritizira političare koji postaju nedosljedni kada dođu na vlast. Brani Mostovu dosljednost, ističući da Most nije 'doveo HDZ na vlast', već je HDZ sam srušio prvu vladu jer Most nije želio žmiriti na nečasne radnje. Također, naglašava da Mostovi ministri 2016. godine nisu htjeli podržati Zdravka Marića zbog sukoba interesa s Todorićem, držeći se moralnih standarda. Smatra da je Mostova dosljednost ključna za njegov opstanak i relevantnost, jer pokazuje da im nije najvažnija 'fotelja', već principi.
sections.subtitle_en: Resistance of the ruling to changes of the electoral law
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović explains why HDZ and SDP do not want to change the electoral law and defends Most's consistency in political action.
sections.content_en: Ivan Orlović explains that for large parties, such as **HDZ and SDP**, the current electoral system is suitable because it has been tailored in their favor since the nineties. He believes it would be 'foolish' to change something that brings them good results. Orlović lists two missed opportunities for changing the electoral law: the first was an initiative by an association (in which Zvonimir Troskot also participated before his political career) for a referendum on changing the electoral law (two-three preferential votes, a 3% threshold), which failed due to the Constitutional Court's decision on 'illegible signatures', which he calls 'the theft of the referendum'. The second opportunity was in 2020 when Most invited Domovinski pokret not to coalesce with HDZ, but to form a parliamentary majority for changing the electoral law and early elections, which Domovinski pokret did not accept. Orlović criticizes politicians who become inconsistent when they come to power. He defends Most's consistency, pointing out that Most did not 'bring HDZ to power', but HDZ itself overthrew the first government because Most did not want to turn a blind eye to dishonorable actions. Also, he emphasizes that Most's ministers in 2016 did not want to support Zdravko Marić due to a conflict of interest with Todorić, adhering to moral standards. He believes that Most's consistency is crucial for its survival and relevance, because it shows that the 'chair' is not most important to them, but principles.

sections.subtitle: Uloga bankarskog sektora i suverenistička politika
sections.screenshot_description: Matija i Ivan Orlović raspravljaju o utjecaju privatnog bankarskog sektora na građane i mogućnostima suverenističke ekonomske politike.
sections.content: Matija je pokrenuo raspravu o ulozi bankarskog sektora u kontekstu Mostove suverenističke politike. Postavio je pitanje zašto bankarstvo, kao pokretač ekonomije, nije javna usluga poput odvoza smeća, sugerirajući da privatni bankarski sektor 'cijedi' najsiromašnije građane. Objasnio je da država prikuplja fiskalni novac, ali građani mogu posuđivati novac samo od komercijalnih banaka, a ne izravno od države, što stvara 'cirkularnu ekonomiju' gdje građani posuđuju vlastiti novac uz kamate koje završavaju u džepovima velikih igrača. Matija je kritizirao i Hrvatsku poštansku banku (HPB) zbog nedostatka patriotskog ponašanja. Ivan Orlović se složio da su banke postale 'prebahate' i da negativno utječu na sudbinu malog čovjeka, posebno u odobravanju kredita. Iako nije ekonomist, smatra da je nenormalno da komercijalne banke jednim klikom određuju sudbinu mladih obitelji. Prisjetio se vremena kada je Hrvatska imala domaće banke i vjeruje da bi povratak takvom sustavu bio bolji, ali za to je potrebna politička moć. Nada se da će se ljudi 'politički preodgojiti' i shvatiti da postoje bolje opcije.
sections.subtitle_en: Role of the banking sector and sovereignist policy
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matija and Ivan Orlović discuss about the influence of the private banking sector on citizens and the possibilities of sovereignist economic policy.
sections.content_en: Matija initiated a discussion about the role of the banking sector in the context of Most's sovereignist policy. He raised the question of why banking, as an economy driver, is not a public service like waste collection, suggesting that the private banking sector 'squeezes' the poorest citizens. He explained that the state collects fiscal money, but citizens can borrow money only from commercial banks, and not directly from the state, which creates a 'circular economy' where citizens borrow their own money with interest that ends up in the pockets of big players. Matija also criticized Hrvatska poštanska banka (HPB) for its lack of patriotic behavior. Ivan Orlović agreed that banks have become 'too arrogant' and negatively affect the fate of the common person, especially in approving loans. Although he is not an economist, he believes it is abnormal that commercial banks determine the fate of young families with a single click. He recalled a time when Croatia had domestic banks and believes that a return to such a system would be better, but political power is needed for that. He hopes that people will 'politically re-educate themselves' and realize that better options exist.

sections.subtitle: Preferencijalni glasovi i nezavisne liste
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović objašnjava izazove okupljanja nezavisnih lista i važnost ideološke homogenosti za nacionalnu politiku.
sections.content: Matija je primijetio da je na zadnjim parlamentarnim izborima jedan zastupnik ušao u Sabor s nešto više od 4.500 preferencijalnih glasova, što sugerira da je relativno mali broj glasova dovoljan za ulazak. Ivan Orlović je potvrdio da, iako se čini malo, osvojiti 4-5 tisuća preferencijalnih glasova nije lako, s obzirom na velik broj kandidata i stranaka u svakoj izbornoj jedinici. Zatim se osvrnuo na koncept nezavisnih lista, podsjetivši da je Most i sam počeo kao 'Most nezavisnih lista'. Međutim, Orlović smatra da takve politike **ne mogu dugoročno egzistirati na nacionalnoj razini** u ozbiljnom smislu. Problem je u tome što okupljanje nezavisnih lista često rezultira ideološkom heterogenošću. Iako to može funkcionirati do određene razine, prije ili poslije ideološke teme postaju ključne i dovode do rascijepa. Most je to, kako kaže, prošao i zaključuje da je nemoguće voditi ozbiljnu politiku na državnoj razini ako stranka nije ideološki homogena, bez obzira na to je li centristička, desna, lijeva ili liberalna.
sections.subtitle_en: Preferential votes and independent lists
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović explains the challenges of gathering independent lists and the importance of ideological homogeneity for national politics.
sections.content_en: Matija noticed that in the last parliamentary elections, one representative entered the Sabor with slightly more than 4,500 preferential votes, which suggests that a relatively small number of votes is sufficient for entry. Ivan Orlović confirmed that, although it seems small, winning 4-5 thousand preferential votes is not easy, given the large number of candidates and parties in each electoral unit. He then referred to the concept of independent lists, reminding that Most itself started as 'Most nezavisnih lista'. However, Orlović believes that such policies **cannot exist long-term at the national level** in a serious sense. The problem is that the gathering of independent lists often results in ideological heterogeneity. Although this can function up to a certain level, sooner or later ideological topics become crucial and lead to a split. Most, as he says, went through this and concludes that it is impossible to conduct serious politics at the state level if a party is not ideologically homogeneous, regardless of whether it is centrist, right-wing, left-wing, or liberal.

sections.subtitle: Balans ideoloških i egzistencijalnih tema
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović naglašava važnost pronalaska ravnoteže između ideoloških i egzistencijalnih tema u političkom djelovanju.
sections.content: Matija je istaknuo da su ideološke teme najvažnije jer izazivaju emocije. Ivan Orlović se složio s njihovom važnošću, ali je kritizirao političare koji zloupotrebljavaju emocije za 'jeftine političke poene' bez sadržaja. Primjećuje da mlađi konzervativni birači postaju zasićeni političarima koji koriste ideološke teme za dobivanje glasova, a zatim ne opravdaju povjerenje. Orlović vjeruje da političar i politička opcija moraju pronaći **balans između ideoloških i egzistencijalnih tema**. Most, kako navodi, jasno stoji iza svojih svjetonazorskih pitanja poput Domovinskog rata, obitelji, braka i konzervativnih kršćanskih vrijednosti. Međutim, ne bave se samo time, već i egzistencijalnim temama, poput zaštite gotovine Ustavom, kako bi se spriječila potpuna digitalna kontrola nad financijama. Povlači paralelu sa slobodom izbora tijekom pandemije, naglašavajući važnost poštivanja individualnog izbora. Zaključuje da je najbitniji balans između svjetonazorskih i egzistencijalnih tema, te da političke opcije ne bi smjele ignorirati nijednu od njih, već imati jasan stav i osjetiti trenutak.
sections.subtitle_en: Balance of ideological and existential themes
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović emphasizes the importance of finding a balance between ideological and existential themes in political action.
sections.content_en: Matija pointed out that ideological topics are the most important because they evoke emotions. Ivan Orlović agreed with their importance, but criticized politicians who misuse emotions for 'cheap political points' without substance. He notices that younger conservative voters are becoming saturated with politicians who use ideological topics to gain votes, and then do not justify trust. Orlović believes that a politician and a political option must find a **balance between ideological and existential topics**. Most, as he states, clearly stands behind its worldview issues such as the Homeland War, family, marriage, and conservative Christian values. However, they do not deal only with that, but also with existential topics, such as the protection of cash by the Constitution, in order to prevent complete digital control over finances. He draws a parallel with freedom of choice during the pandemic, emphasizing the importance of respecting individual choice. He concludes that the most important thing is the balance between worldview and existential topics, and that political options should not ignore any of them, but rather have a clear stance and feel the moment.

iterations.theme_en: The role of Most in Croatian politics and challenges of the electoral system

iterations.theme: Angažman građana, ekonomska pitanja i budući izazovi Hrvatske

sections.subtitle: Politika kao sveprisutna sila: Zašto pasivnost građana šteti društvu?
sections.screenshot_description: Matija i Ivan Orlović razgovaraju o važnosti politike i pasivnosti građana, s naglaskom na to kako politika utječe na svakodnevni život.
sections.content: Voditelj Matija ističe kako bi politika trebala biti područje rezervirano za najplemenitije ljude u državi, s obzirom na to da je ona sveprisutna i utječe na svaki aspekt života – od najsitnijih komunalnih odluka poput crtanja zebri na ulici do složenih pitanja odvoza smeća. Gost Ivan Orlović, organizacijski tajnik Mosta, snažno se slaže s tom tvrdnjom, izražavajući svoje nerazumijevanje za pasivnost građana koji često izjavljuju da ih politika ne zanima. Orlović naglašava da upravo ta pasivnost i nezainteresiranost dovode do daljnjeg politikanstva i neučinkovitog upravljanja. Smatra da bi značajno veća izlaznost na izbore, primjerice 90% umjesto trenutnih 60%, te aktivno sudjelovanje svakog pojedinca, počevši od lokalne razine mjesnih zajednica, drastično poboljšalo cjelokupnu političku i društvenu situaciju u zemlji.
sections.subtitle_en: Politics as an omnipresent force: Why citizen passivity harms society?
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matija and Ivan Orlović discuss the importance of politics and the passivity of citizens, with an emphasis on how politics affects everyday life.
sections.content_en: Host Matija points out that politics should be an area reserved for the noblest people in the country, given that it is omnipresent and affects every aspect of life – from the smallest communal decisions like drawing crosswalks on the street to complex issues of waste disposal. Guest Ivan Orlović, organizational secretary of Most, strongly agrees with that statement, expressing his incomprehension for the passivity of citizens who often declare that politics does not interest them. Orlović emphasizes that precisely this passivity and disinterest lead to further politicking and inefficient governance. He believes that a significantly higher voter turnout, for example 90% instead of the current 60%, and active participation of every individual, starting from the local level of local communities, would drastically improve the overall political and social situation in the country.

sections.subtitle: Niska izlaznost i 'fantomski birači': Odraz naroda u parlamentu
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović objašnjava kako niska izlaznost na izbore utječe na kvalitetu vlasti, dok Matija spominje problem 'fantomskih birača'.
sections.content: Matija se poziva na tezu profesora Mužića, koji tvrdi da je parlament izravan odraz naroda, te da loši političari na vlasti predstavljaju svojevrsnu kaznu narodu zbog njegove nezrelosti. Ivan Orlović se u potpunosti slaže s ovom perspektivom, ističući da su sve dosadašnje vlasti u Hrvatskoj legitimno izabrane od strane građana. Kao ilustrativan primjer navodi situaciju u Zagrebu, gdje je izlaznost na gradonačelničkim izborima bila alarmantno niska, svega 38%. To znači da čak 62% građana nije iskoristilo svoje biračko pravo, a mnogi od njih su upravo oni koji se kasnije najglasnije žale na rad gradonačelnika Tomaševića. Matija dodatno produbljuje analizu, navodeći podatke s prošlih parlamentarnih izbora, gdje je za osvajanje 77 mandata bilo potrebno 1.7 milijuna glasova, dok se čak 1.39 milijuna ljudi nije pojavilo na biralištima. Posebno naglašava problem 'fantomskih birača' i prenapuhanog broja birača u Hrvatskoj, koji je nerealno visok u odnosu na ukupan broj stanovnika, što dodatno dovodi u pitanje integritet izbornog procesa.
sections.subtitle_en: Low turnout and 'phantom voters': Reflection of the people in parliament
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović explains how low voter turnout affects the quality of government, while Matija mentions the problem of 'phantom voters'.
sections.content_en: Matija refers to the thesis of Professor Mužić, who claims that parliament is a direct reflection of the people, and that bad politicians in power represent a kind of punishment for the people due to their immaturity. Ivan Orlović completely agrees with this perspective, emphasizing that all previous governments in Croatia were legitimately elected by the citizens. As an illustrative example, he cites the situation in Zagreb, where voter turnout in the mayoral elections was alarmingly low, only 38%. This means that as many as 62% of citizens did not exercise their right to vote, and many of them are precisely those who later complain most loudly about the work of Mayor Tomašević. Matija further deepens the analysis, citing data from past parliamentary elections, where 1.7 million votes were needed to win 77 mandates, while as many as 1.39 million people did not appear at the polling stations. He particularly emphasizes the problem of 'phantom voters' and the inflated number of voters in Croatia, which is unrealistically high in relation to the total population, which further calls into question the integrity of the electoral process.

sections.subtitle: Online i dopisno glasovanje: Mostovi prijedlozi za transparentnije izbore
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović govori o prijedlozima Mosta za uvođenje online i dopisnog glasovanja te kritizira bojkot izbora.
sections.content: Ivan Orlović, unatoč osobnom iskustvu u izbornom povjerenstvu, izražava mišljenje da trenutni sustav glasovanja u Hrvatskoj pogoduje vladajućim strukturama. Most je aktivno predlagao uvođenje online ili dopisnog glasovanja, navodeći primjer Bosne i Hercegovine gdje je takav sustav, unatoč općoj nefunkcionalnosti države, manje podložan malverzacijama. Orlović smatra da vladajućima odgovara niska izlaznost jer se njihovi glasači ionako mobiliziraju, dok bi online glasovanje značajno povećalo izlaznost i smanjilo mogućnost manipulacija. Iako vjeruje da malverzacija u izbornom procesu ima, ne smatra da one bitno utječu na konačne rezultate. Kritizira bojkot izbora, ističući da je izlazak na biralište i prekrižavanje listića, čime se poništava glas, ipak snažna poruka, dok je neizlazak najgora opcija jer se time prepušta drugima da odlučuju. Podsjeća na grčki pojam 'idiotas', koji se u staroj Grčkoj koristio za one koji nisu konzumirali svoje biračko pravo. Savjetuje građanima da izađu na izbore i ponište listić kako bi spriječili potencijalne malverzacije s njihovim neiskorištenim glasačkim listićima, čiji serijski brojevi mogu biti zloupotrijebljeni.
sections.subtitle_en: Online and postal voting: Most's proposals for more transparent elections
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović speaks about Most's proposals for the introduction of online and postal voting and criticizes the boycott of elections.
sections.content_en: Ivan Orlović, despite personal experience in the electoral commission, expresses the opinion that the current voting system in Croatia favors the ruling structures. Most actively proposed the introduction of online or postal voting, citing the example of Bosnia and Herzegovina where such a system, despite the general dysfunctionality of the state, is less susceptible to malversations. Orlović believes that low turnout suits the ruling parties because their voters are mobilized anyway, while online voting would significantly increase turnout and reduce the possibility of manipulations. Although he believes that there are malversations in the electoral process, he does not consider that they significantly affect the final results. He criticizes the boycott of elections, pointing out that going to the polling station and crossing out the ballot, thereby invalidating the vote, is still a strong message, while not voting is the worst option because it leaves others to decide. He recalls the Greek term 'idiotas', which in ancient Greece was used for those who did not exercise their right to vote. He advises citizens to go to the elections and invalidate their ballot to prevent potential malversations with their unused ballots, whose serial numbers can be misused.

sections.subtitle: Mostova strategija: Kampanja koja traje četiri godine
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović objašnjava kako Most pristupa političkoj kampanji kao kontinuiranom procesu, a ne samo predizbornoj aktivnosti.
sections.content: Voditelj Matija kritizira uobičajenu praksu političkih stranaka koje se intenzivno pojavljuju u javnom prostoru tek dva tjedna prije izbora, sugerirajući da bi politička kampanja trebala biti kontinuirani proces koji traje pune četiri godine. U tom kontekstu, Matija pohvaljuje Most zbog iznimne prisutnosti u medijima i na društvenim mrežama. Ivan Orlović se u potpunosti slaže s tom ocjenom, objašnjavajući da, prema načelima političkog marketinga, kampanja zapravo počinje dan nakon izbora. Naglašava da se Most, unatoč ograničenim resursima i mogućnostima, dosljedno trudi biti stalno prisutan u javnosti. Kao dokaz te tvrdnje, Orlović navodi javno dostupne podatke koji pokazuju da su Mostovi zastupnici već tri mandata zaredom najaktivniji u Saboru, s najvećim brojem zakonskih prijedloga, amandmana i javnih govora. Time potvrđuje da Most svoju kampanju shvaća kao svakodnevni rad, a ne samo kao kratkotrajnu predizbornu aktivnost.
sections.subtitle_en: Most's strategy: Campaign which lasts four years
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović explains how Most approaches a political campaign as a continuous process, and not just a pre-election activity.
sections.content_en: Host Matija criticizes the usual practice of political parties that intensely appear in the public space only two weeks before elections, suggesting that a political campaign should be a continuous process lasting a full four years. In that context, Matija praises Most for its exceptional presence in the media and on social networks. Ivan Orlović completely agrees with that assessment, explaining that, according to the principles of political marketing, a campaign actually begins the day after the elections. He emphasizes that Most, despite limited resources and opportunities, consistently strives to be constantly present in the public. As proof of that claim, Orlović cites publicly available data showing that Most's representatives have been the most active in Sabor for three consecutive terms, with the highest number of legislative proposals, amendments, and public speeches. This confirms that Most understands its campaign as daily work, and not just as a short-term pre-election activity.

sections.subtitle: Samoodrživost u hrani i energetici: Prioriteti za budućnost Hrvatske
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović govori o ključnim temama za Hrvatsku, ističući važnost samodostatnosti u hrani i energetici.
sections.content: Ivan Orlović ističe da su Mostovi zastupnici uspješno profilirani u različitim sektorima, gdje svaki od njih doprinosi svojim specifičnim znanjem i iskustvom. Kao primjer navodi Antu Kujundžića, koji se istaknuo u socijalnim pitanjima, palijativnoj skrbi te brizi za starije, nemoćne i djecu. Prema Orlovićevom osobnom mišljenju, najvažnije teme za Hrvatsku u ovom trenutku su **samoodrživost u hrani i energetici**. Naglašava apsurd da Hrvatska, unatoč bogatstvu prirodnih resursa, uvozi značajne količine energije i hrane, posebno ukazujući na neobrađena polja u Slavoniji. Kao pozitivan primjer samodostatnosti navodi Austriju, koja je, kako kaže, spremna deset godina ne uvoziti ništa, ni hranu, ni energiju, ni materijale. Orlović smatra da je suludo uvoziti svinje, krave, mlijeko i jaja, te da je samodostatnost u hrani i energetici tema broj jedan, uz demografiju kao najbitniju dugoročnu temu za opstanak nacije.
sections.subtitle_en: Self-sustainability in food and energy: Priorities for the future of Croatia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović speaks about key topics for Croatia, emphasizing the importance of self-sufficiency in food and energy.
sections.content_en: Ivan Orlović points out that Most's representatives are successfully profiled in various sectors, where each of them contributes with their specific knowledge and experience. As an example, he mentions Ante Kujundžić, who distinguished himself in social issues, palliative care, and care for the elderly, infirm, and children. According to Orlović's personal opinion, the most important topics for Croatia at this moment are **self-sustainability in food and energy**. He emphasizes the absurdity that Croatia, despite its wealth of natural resources, imports significant quantities of energy and food, especially pointing to uncultivated fields in Slavonija. As a positive example of self-sufficiency, he cites Austria, which, as he says, is ready for ten years not to import anything, neither food, nor energy, nor materials. Orlović believes that it is absurd to import pigs, cows, milk, and eggs, and that self-sufficiency in food and energy is topic number one, with demography as the most important long-term topic for the survival of the nation.

sections.subtitle: Strane investicije i zaštita OPG-ova: Borba protiv 'mutnih' ulaganja
sections.screenshot_description: Ivan Orlović objašnjava stav Mosta o stranim investicijama, naglašavajući potrebu zaštite domaćih OPG-ova.
sections.content: Matija postavlja pitanje o stranim investicijama i razlozima zašto ih suverenističke opcije često percipiraju kao problem. Ivan Orlović objašnjava da problem nije u samim investicijama, već u pozadini tih investitora, koji su često 'mutni' i koriste podmićivanje te korupciju kako bi dobili potrebne dozvole. Kao ilustrativan primjer navodi priču o planiranoj klaonici kokoši u Sisačko-moslavačkoj županiji, koja bi trošila enormne količine pitke vode i time ugrozila male OPG-ove. Orlović jasno ističe da Most nije protiv legalnih investicija koje doprinose boljitku Hrvatske, već je protiv uništavanja malih gospodarstvenika i 'mutnih' investicija koje služe za pranje novca. Dodaje da je odgovornost države da rastereti male obrtnike i poduzetnike od prevelikih nameta, navodeći udrugu Glas poduzetnika koja se stalno žali na visoka davanja. Matija se nadovezuje na ideju udruživanja OPG-ova u zadruge kako bi se postigla bolja konkurentnost na tržištu, spominjući Zadrugu za etično financiranje kao primjer pokušaja uspostave cirkularne ekonomije.
sections.subtitle_en: Foreign investments and protection of family farms: Fight against 'shady' investments
sections.screenshot_description_en: Ivan Orlović explains Most's position on foreign investments, emphasizing the need for the protection of domestic family farms.
sections.content_en: Matija asks a question about foreign investments and the reasons why sovereignist options often perceive them as a problem. Ivan Orlović explains that the problem is not in the investments themselves, but in the background of these investors, who are often 'shady' and use bribery and corruption to obtain the necessary permits. As an illustrative example, he cites the story of a planned chicken slaughterhouse in Sisačko-moslavačka County, which would consume enormous quantities of drinking water and thereby endanger small OPG-ove. Orlović clearly points out that Most is not against legal investments that contribute to the betterment of Croatia, but is against the destruction of small entrepreneurs and 'shady' investments that serve for money laundering. He adds that it is the state's responsibility to relieve small artisans and entrepreneurs from excessive burdens, citing the association Glas poduzetnika which constantly complains about high levies. Matija builds on the idea of OPG-ove uniting into cooperatives to achieve better market competitiveness, mentioning Zadrugu za etično financiranje as an example of an attempt to establish a circular economy.

sections.subtitle: Porezna politika i bankarski sektor: Kritika APN-a i uloge HPB-a
sections.screenshot_description: Matija i Ivan Orlović razgovaraju o poreznoj politici i bankarskom sektoru, s posebnim osvrtom na APN kredite i ulogu Hrvatske poštanske banke.
sections.content: Matija i Ivan Orlović nastavljaju razgovor o poreznoj politici i bankarskom sektoru. Matija ističe porez na dohodak kao dobar primjer cirkularne ekonomije, gdje novac završava u lokalnoj zajednici i financira javne usluge, poput besplatnih vrtića u Velikoj Gorici. Orlović se slaže, spominjući i Sinj kao grad s besplatnim vrtićima, ali naglašava da su nameti i davanja države prema poduzetnicima i gospodarstvenicima i dalje preveliki. Matija kritizira bankarski sektor, posebno u kontekstu APN kredita. Iako je APN državni novac namijenjen za pomoć građanima pri kupnji nekretnina, Matija smatra suludim da Hrvatska poštanska banka (HPB), kao hrvatska banka, nije imala najbolje uvjete za APN kredite. Navodi svoj osobni primjer gdje je morao uzeti kredit u PBZ-u (koji nije hrvatska banka) jer je nudio bolje uvjete (fiksnu kamatu od 1.99% na 20 godina). Smatra da su svi APN krediti trebali biti plasirani isključivo kroz HPB kako bi se potaknula domaća ekonomija i osigurali najbolji uvjeti za hrvatske građane, čime bi se izbjeglo da državni novac završava u rukama stranih banaka.
sections.subtitle_en: Tax policy and the banking sector: Critique of APN and the role of HPB
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matija and Ivan Orlović discuss tax policy and the banking sector, with a special focus on APN loans and the role of Hrvatska poštanska banka.
sections.content_en: Matija and Ivan Orlović continue the conversation about tax policy and the banking sector. Matija highlights income tax as a good example of a circular economy, where money ends up in the local community and finances public services, such as free kindergartens in Velika Gorica. Orlović agrees, also mentioning Sinj as a city with free kindergartens, but emphasizes that state levies and contributions towards entrepreneurs and business owners are still too high. Matija criticizes the banking sector, especially in the context of APN loans. Although APN is state money intended to help citizens with real estate purchases, Matija considers it absurd that Hrvatska poštanska banka (HPB), as a Croatian bank, did not have the best conditions for APN loans. He cites his personal example where he had to take a loan from PBZ (which is not a Croatian bank) because it offered better conditions (a fixed interest rate of 1.99% for 20 years). He believes that all APN loans should have been placed exclusively through HPB to stimulate the domestic economy and ensure the best conditions for Croatian citizens, thereby avoiding state money ending up in the hands of foreign banks.

sections.subtitle: Poziv na aktivno sudjelovanje: Glasajte po savjesti za pravednu Hrvatsku
sections.screenshot_description: Matija i Ivan Orlović zaključuju razgovor pozivom građanima na aktivno sudjelovanje na izborima i glasanje po savjesti.
sections.content: Matija zaključuje razgovor zahvaljujući Ivanu Orloviću na dolasku i izdvojenom vremenu. Ponovno upućuje snažan poziv građanima na aktivno sudjelovanje na nadolazećim izborima. Naglašava da nije važno za koga će glasati, već da je ključno izaći na birališta i pronaći najispravniju osobu na listi, glasajući isključivo po svojoj savjesti. Matija ističe da čak i ako ta osoba ne uđe u Sabor, građani znaju da su dali glas svojoj savjesti, čime njihova savjest ostaje čista. Izražava uvjerenje da će se, kada svi građani tako postupe i glasaju za pravedne pojedince, s vremenom pravedni ljudi naći u Saboru i voditi državu. Ivan Orlović se u potpunosti slaže s ovom porukom, naglašavajući važnost individualne političke odgovornosti.
sections.subtitle_en: Call to active participation: Vote according to conscience for a just Croatia
sections.screenshot_description_en: Matija and Ivan Orlović conclude the conversation with an invitation to citizens for active participation in the elections and voting according to conscience.
sections.content_en: Matija concludes the conversation thanking Ivan Orlović for coming and for the time dedicated. He again directs a strong call to citizens for active participation in the upcoming elections. He emphasizes that it is not important for whom they will vote, but that it is crucial to go to the polls and find the most righteous person on the list, voting exclusively according to their conscience. Matija points out that even if that person does not enter the Sabor, citizens know that they have given their vote to their conscience, whereby their conscience remains clean. He expresses conviction that, when all citizens act in this way and vote for righteous individuals, over time righteous people will be found in the Sabor and will lead the state. Ivan Orlović completely agrees with this message, emphasizing the importance of individual political responsibility.

iterations.theme_en: Citizen engagement, economic issues and future challenges of Croatia
